title.en: EUDAMED-MDR/IVDR Ontology for Medical Devices and In-Vitro Diagnostics

description.en: The ontology models the European catalog of high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices in accordance with the MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746) regulations. It represents the concepts that underpin the domain: devices, risk classes, manufacturers, identified risks (using IMDRF nomenclature), reported incidents, and field safety corrective actions (FSCA). It draws on data from EUDAMED, and its design aims to facilitate semantic interoperability among manufacturers, regulatory authorities, hospitals, and patients.

title.value: EUDAMED-MDR/IVDR Ontology for Medical Devices and In-Vitro Diagnostics

comment.en: Risk that the device fails to perform as intended, potentially leading to delayed treatment or harm.
Risk of microbial or chemical contamination of the device, potentially causing infection or adverse health effects.
An incident that resulted in death, serious deterioration in health, or a life‑threatening situation.
An event that could have led to harm but did not, often due to timely intervention or chance.
Risk that the device causes or is affected by electromagnetic or other interference, compromising its performance.
A device used to examine specimens derived from the human body to provide information for diagnostic or monitoring purposes (IVDR 2017/746).
A potential hazard associated with a medical device, classified according to the IMDRF nomenclature.
Risk associated with software bugs, inadequate functionality, or cybersecurity flaws in the device's software.
A low‑risk medical device under MDR 2017/745, typically subject to self‑declaration of conformity.
Risk related to the materials used in the device, such as toxicity or biocompatibility issues.
Risk of excessive or unintended radiation exposure from the device, leading to tissue damage or other health effects.
Risk that the device causes or contributes to infection, particularly in implantable or reusable devices.
A device intended to be placed into the human body, either partially or wholly, and remains after the procedure.
A communication to users (e.g., healthcare providers, patients) providing instructions or warnings regarding device use.
A high‑risk medical device under MDR 2017/745, requiring the highest level of scrutiny and clinical evaluation.
A safety measure taken to address a device-related incident, including field safety corrective actions (FSCA) and recalls.
Risk associated with incorrect or delayed diagnosis due to device failure or misinterpretation of results.
A device that does not rely on electrical or mechanical energy to function, such as bandages or surgical instruments.
A device that depends on a source of energy (e.g., electrical, mechanical) to perform its function, excluding energy generated by the human body.
Removal or correction of a device that is in violation of regulations or poses a safety risk, conducted by the manufacturer.
Risk arising from vulnerabilities in the device's software or network connectivity that could lead to unauthorized access or malfunction.
An event that has resulted or could result in harm to a patient, user, or other person, associated with a medical device.
A corrective action performed directly on the device at the point of use, such as a software update or hardware adjustment.
An urgent notification issued to inform users about a serious safety issue and to recommend specific actions.
Risk of physical harm or injury to the patient or user due to device failure or misuse.
A medium‑high risk medical device under MDR 2017/745, subject to strict conformity assessment procedures.
A medium‑risk medical device under MDR 2017/745, with moderate conformity assessment requirements.
Relates an organization to the medical devices it produces. This is the inverse of eudamed:hasManufacturer.
A textual description of the consequences of the incident, such as patient harm or device malfunction.
The device risk class according to the regulatory framework (e.g., Class I, IIa, IIb, III for MDR; Class A, B, C, D for IVDR).
The current status of a corrective action (e.g., Open, Closed).
Indicates that a device is impacted by a specific incident. This is the inverse of eudamed:affectsDevice.
The estimated likelihood of the risk occurring (e.g., Low, Medium, High).
The applicable regulation under which the device is placed on the market, such as MDR or IVDR.
Identifies the organization that submitted an incident report.
The unique reference number assigned to a Field Safety Corrective Action (FSCA) in the EUDAMED database.
The Unique Device Identifier - Device Identifier (UDI-DI) which serves as the primary identifier for a medical device model.
The sex of the patient involved in the incident.
The code from the International Medical Device Regulators Forum (IMDRF) that classifies the type of medical risk.
The category of risk, such as Device Failure, Patient Harm, or Diagnostic Error.
A boolean flag indicating whether the device is an active medical device (dependent on a source of energy).
The country from which an incident was reported, derived from the reporting organization's address.
A general classification of the medical device, e.g., Implantable Active Device, In-Vitro Diagnostic.
Indicates the medical device that is the subject of an incident report.
Links a corrective action to the incident that it addresses.
A textual description of the scope of a corrective action, detailing which devices or batches are included.
A boolean flag indicating whether a corrective action has been taken in response to an incident.
The date on which the incident occurred.
The official authorization number or registration number assigned to an organization by a competent authority.
A boolean flag indicating whether the device is intended to be partially or wholly introduced into the human body.
The number of devices impacted by a corrective action.
The intended use or clinical function of the medical device as defined by the manufacturer.
The age of the patient involved in the incident.
The countries where a corrective action is applicable.
A boolean flag indicating whether the device can be reused after appropriate processing.
A more specific classification of the risk, e.g., Malfunction Risk, Infection Risk, or Diagnostic Risk.
The level of severity of the medical risk (e.g., Critical, Serious, Moderate).
The date on which an incident was detected.
The name of the national competent authority responsible for the organization.
A boolean flag indicating whether the device is an In-Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) medical device.
The unique Single Registration Number (SRN) assigned to an economic operator in the EUDAMED system.
Associates a medical device with the organization that manufactured it.
The classification of the incident, e.g., serious incident or near-miss.
Relates an organization to the incident reports it has submitted. This is the inverse of eudamed:reportedBy.
Links an incident to the medical risk associated with it.
The unique reference number assigned to an incident report in the EUDAMED database.
The type of corrective action taken, such as a safety alert, recall, or in-situ correction.
The type of device that is affected by a particular medical risk.
